roms.name: LineageOS
roms.shortTagline: The largest community-built Android distribution, descended from CyanogenMod.
description: LineageOS is a free and open-source operating system based on Android, with official builds for over 200 devices and a strong focus on customization and longevity.
It is maintained by hundreds of volunteer developers and is widely considered the reference custom ROM for any modern Android device.
features: Officially supports a huge range of devices, including extended security updates.
Built-in PrivacyGuard for per-app permission controls.
Updater app delivers signed OTA updates straight from the official servers.
Optional MicroG, F-Droid, or Google Apps via separate installers.

roms.name: crDroid
roms.shortTagline: LineageOS-based ROM packed with extra customisation knobs.
description: crDroid extends LineageOS with a deep customisation layer covering the lockscreen, status bar, navigation gestures, and theming engine.
Builds track upstream Android security patches monthly and ship for ~150 devices.
features: Status-bar, lockscreen, gesture and theming customisation.
GApps and vanilla variants for most devices.

roms.name: Pixel Experience
roms.shortTagline: AOSP-based ROM that mirrors the Google Pixel software experience.
description: Pixel Experience ships the Pixel launcher, wallpapers, icons, fonts, boot animation, and Google apps so your device behaves like a Pixel.
Built straight from AOSP and updated alongside Google security patches.
features: Pixel-style UI, animations, and wallpapers out of the box.
Google apps and Pixel exclusive features pre-installed.
Monthly security patches in sync with Google Pixel.
Plus variant adds extra customisation if you want it.

roms.name: ArrowOS
roms.shortTagline: Minimal AOSP-based ROM focused on smoothness and zero bloat.
description: ArrowOS sticks close to stock Android, dropping in only the most-requested customisations to keep things fast and battery friendly.
Ideal if you want something stable, near-vanilla, and easy to live with.
features: Near-stock AOSP with curated quality-of-life additions.
Focus on stability and battery life over feature creep.

roms.name: Evolution X
roms.shortTagline: Pixel-style ROM that adds extra customisation on top of AOSP.
description: Evolution X combines a Pixel-like baseline (launcher, wallpapers, boot animation) with a wide customisation menu and Google apps.
features: Pixel UI baseline with optional GApps included.

roms.name: Paranoid Android
roms.shortTagline: Refined AOSP ROM known for tasteful UI changes and Pixel polish.
description: Paranoid Android is one of the oldest custom ROM projects. It focuses on small, considered UX touches like pop-up notifications and an enhanced ambient display.
features: Pop-up notifications and refined ambient display.
Active community, slow but careful release cadence.

roms.name: Havoc-OS
roms.shortTagline: LineageOS-derived ROM with a rich feature set and Substratum theming.
description: Havoc-OS is built on top of LineageOS device trees and AOSP, bundling a large set of customisation toggles and built-in Substratum theming.
Active development has slowed in recent years; check the official site for current device coverage.

roms.name: DotOS
roms.shortTagline: Stock-like AOSP ROM with a tasteful design language of its own.
description: DotOS focuses on visual polish and small daily-driver improvements rather than maximum customisation.
features: Curated design language across the system UI.

roms.name: Bliss ROMs
roms.shortTagline: LineageOS-based ROM with builds for phones, tablets, and PCs.
description: Bliss ROMs ships LineageOS-based builds for Android handsets and a separate Bliss OS x86 build for PCs and tablets, reusing much of the same UX code.
features: Sister Bliss OS x86 builds for PCs.

roms.name: POSP
roms.shortTagline: AOSP-based "Potato Open Sauce Project" with a light customisation set.
description: POSP ships a clean AOSP base with a curated set of features and a focus on snappy, low-overhead daily use.
features: Pixel launcher and tasteful UI tweaks.

roms.name: RisingOS
roms.shortTagline: Feature-rich Android 14/15 ROM with a strong customisation suite.
description: RisingOS is a newer-generation custom ROM that ships a deep customisation menu, AI integrations, and polished animations on top of AOSP 14 and 15.
features: Rich customisation: lockscreen, status-bar, gestures, themes.

roms.name: Voltage OS
roms.shortTagline: Vanilla-leaning AOSP ROM aimed at battery life and snappiness.
description: Voltage OS keeps very close to AOSP and adds only the minimum set of features to give a clean, fast daily driver.

roms.name: Project Elixir
roms.shortTagline: Modern AOSP ROM with a polished Pixel-inspired UI and rich customisation.
description: Project Elixir is a fast-moving AOSP-based ROM that ships Pixel launcher and animations with an extensive customisation suite.
features: Pixel UI baseline and GApps included.

roms.name: GrapheneOS
roms.shortTagline: Privacy- and security-hardened AOSP ROM for Google Pixel devices.
description: GrapheneOS is a non-profit, open-source mobile OS focused on privacy and security improvements over stock Android.
Builds are AOSP-based and target Google Pixel phones (3a and newer) where the hardware can be re-locked after install.
features: Hardened malloc, MTE, fortified kernel, exec-only memory.
Per-app Network, Sensors, Storage and Contacts permissions.
Sandboxed Google Play layer for app compatibility.

roms.name: CalyxOS
roms.shortTagline: Privacy-focused AOSP ROM with microG and verified boot, by The Calyx Institute.
description: CalyxOS is built by The Calyx Institute as a privacy and security focused Android distribution targeting Google Pixel devices and a small set of Fairphone hardware.
It ships with microG pre-configured for de-Googled push, and includes Datura firewall, Signal, and the Aurora Store out of the box.
features: microG bundled and ready, no Google services required.
Verified boot supported on official Pixel targets.
Datura firewall and per-app network controls.
F-Droid, Aurora Store and Signal pre-installed.

roms.name: /e/OS
roms.shortTagline: LineageOS-based, de-Googled OS by the e Foundation (Murena).
description: /e/OS is a privacy-respecting fork of LineageOS that ships microG instead of Google services, a custom launcher, and the e Foundation cloud ecosystem.
Builds are available for a wide range of devices, and Murena also sells pre-installed phones (Fairphone, Pixel, Murena ONE).
features: Bundled App Lounge with anonymous logins and privacy scores.
Official builds for 200+ devices via the e installer.

roms.name: DivestOS
roms.shortTagline: Security-hardened LineageOS soft-fork with extended device support.
description: DivestOS is a soft-fork of LineageOS focused on increased privacy and security, including monthly maintenance for many older devices that upstream has dropped.
Builds ship with F-Droid, Mulch (a hardened Chromium), and additional hardening backported from GrapheneOS.
features: Extended life for legacy devices Lineage no longer ships.
Per-network MAC randomisation and other privacy tweaks.

roms.name: DerpFest
roms.shortTagline: Feature-rich LineageOS-based ROM built by a community of maintainers.
description: DerpFest is a community-driven ROM that layers a deep customisation suite on top of LineageOS, with active maintainers across a broad device list.
Builds are produced for current Android branches and emphasise daily-driver stability.
features: LineageOS base with an extensive customisation menu.

recoveries.name: TWRP
recoveries.shortTagline: The classic Team Win Recovery Project. Touch-based and supported on hundreds of devices.
description: TWRP is the most widely used custom recovery for Android. It exposes a touch-based interface for flashing ZIPs, taking nandroid backups, mounting partitions, and using ADB sideload.
features: ADB sideload, terminal, and file manager.
Officially supported on hundreds of devices.

recoveries.name: OrangeFox Recovery
recoveries.shortTagline: TWRP fork focused on MIUI/Treble decryption and a modern UI.
description: OrangeFox is a fork of TWRP that ships first-class support for MIUI and Treble devices, a refreshed UI, and built-in tools like a Magisk installer.
features: Improved decryption support on Xiaomi/MIUI devices.
Built-in Magisk installer, file manager, terminal.

recoveries.name: RedWolf Recovery
recoveries.shortTagline: TWRP-based recovery with extra MIUI-friendly features.
description: RedWolf Recovery focuses on Xiaomi and OnePlus devices, adding MIUI-style features on top of a TWRP base.

recoveries.name: PitchBlack Recovery
recoveries.shortTagline: TWRP-based recovery built around a dark theme and quality-of-life extras.
description: PitchBlack Recovery Project is a TWRP fork that ships a dark theme out of the box plus quick boot options, MIUI-friendly partition handling, and other QoL features.
features: TWRP base with a curated dark theme.

recoveries.name: SHRP
recoveries.shortTagline: SkyHawk Recovery Project: a TWRP fork with a polished UI and stock-ROM backup helpers.
description: SHRP (formerly SkyHawk Recovery) is a TWRP fork that targets MIUI and OxygenOS devices with a polished UI and built-in stock-rom backup helpers.
